name: AI Job Search
tagline: A job hunt, run as an agent workflow
description: A fork-and-fill framework built on Claude Code: populate a profile, and structured workflows search job portals, score fit, tailor LaTeX CVs and cover letters, prep interviews, and run skill-gap analysis.
why: A clean example of the 'coding CLI as application platform' pattern — an agent product delivered as a repo template rather than a SaaS. The Danish portal integrations show how these templates localize.
useCases: Automating a personal job search end to end
A template for building your own agent workflows on a coding CLI
audience: Job seekers comfortable with Claude Code; template-hunters

setup.requirements: A Claude Code subscription or API access, a LaTeX toolchain for CV output, and a forked repo with your profile filled in

license.notes: The template is MIT, but it drives Claude Code, which is a proprietary, paid tool.

limitations: Danish job portals out of the box (adapt for other markets), a dependency on a paid proprietary agent, and scraping that may brush against portal terms. Treat it as a template, not a product.
take: Less interesting as a job tool than as a genre: personal agent workflows, versioned in git, forked like dotfiles.
